title: CSV, JSON Lines, Schemas, Stable Output, Redaction, and Accessible Reports · Contract Workshop
summary: A public-interest site-quality team incrementally builds versioned machine and accessible human report from original fixed response and HTML fixtures while retaining earlier authorization, HTTP, URL, provenance, politeness, accessibility, testing, security, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of versioned machine and accessible human report while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case p…
objectives: Define UTF-8, newline handling, dialect, ordered fields, quoting, formula policy, missing values, limits, and atomic replacement.
Emit one bounded versioned JSON record per line with stable types, Unicode policy, provenance, and explicit invalid-record handling.
Sort by documented semantic keys and stable tie-breakers so concurrency, retries, and resume do not change comparable output.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Csv Contract
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop site-quality team handles case 8973 during a clean package and recovery drill. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile stable CSV and JSON Lines exports, accessible human reports, provenance, and redaction; automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance; then change malformed markup or parser choice and preserve extraction provenance. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Provenance Redaction
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: scraper-provenance-redaction-1og5w6" that uses case 4616 to reconcile stable CSV and JSON Lines exports, accessible human reports, provenance, and redaction. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, the recorded stable package versions, fixed and malformed fixtures, fake transports, controlled local servers, authorized disposable target sites, representative browser profiles, privacy, security, accessibility and legal review, load, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Provenance Redaction.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Provenance Redaction into observable performance evidence. For Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Provenance Redaction, keep Provenance Redaction connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-provenance-redaction-1og5w6
# Competency: scraper-provenance-redaction.
# Case 4616: a keyboard and screen-reader report review.
# Operating constraint: the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure.
# Changed case: inject one retry, cancellation, queue, checkpoint, or output failure and recover cleanly.
def evidence_scraper_provenance_redaction_1og5w6(valid_records: int = 48, csv_rows: int = 48, jsonl_rows: int = 48, accessible_findings: int = 48, leaked_sensitive_fields: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1og5w6 = "1og5w6-6w5go1-4616"
    assert evidence_variant_1og5w6
    evidence_axes_1og5w6 = (
        "authorization1og5w6",
        "url4616",
        "response6w5go1",
        "tree1og5w64616",
        "frontier46161og5w6",
        "report6w5go14616",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1og5w6)) == 6
    if valid_records < 1 or csv_rows < 0 or jsonl_rows < 0:
        return False, valid_records, "export-count"
    if csv_rows != valid_records or jsonl_rows != valid_records:
        return False, csv_rows, "export-reconciliation"
    if accessible_findings != valid_records:
        return False, accessible_findings, "accessible-report-gap"
    if leaked_sensitive_fields > 0:
        return False, leaked_sensitive_fields, "redaction-gap"
    assert csv_rows == valid_records and jsonl_rows == valid_records
    return True, csv_rows, "stable-accessible-reports"

options.text: Keep the plausible Provenance Redaction output and hide the violated versioned machine and accessible human report assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Provenance Redaction.

For test an acceptance boundary, crawl one convenient page, omit owner authorization, revision, URL and robots policy, response budgets, parser and selector limits, provenance, frontier and concurrency evidence, changed fixtures, privacy, security, accessibility, stop, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as crawler proof. This does not satisfy Provenance Redaction for versioned machine and accessible human report.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Provenance Redaction condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
